title: got wrong result when enable `tidb_opt_agg_push_down`
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)
1. deploy a cluster 
2. prepare tpch data with 1sf `tiup bench tpch prepare -T 10`
3. execute q13
```sql
set tidb_opt_agg_push_down=ON;
SELECT c_count, count(*) as custdist
from ( SELECT c_custkey, count(o_orderkey)  as  c_count
       from customer left join orders on c_custkey = o_custkey and o_comment not like '%special%requests%'
       group by c_custkey ) c_orders
group by c_count
order by custdist desc, c_count desc;
```
<!-- a step by step guide for reproducing the bug. -->

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)
```sql
[17:41:41]TiDB root:test> SELECT c_count, count(*) as custdist
                       -> from ( SELECT c_custkey, count(o_orderkey)  as  c_count
                       ->        from customer left join orders on c_custkey = o_custkey and o_comment not like '%special%requests%'
                       ->        group by c_custkey ) c_orders
                       -> group by c_count
                       -> order by custdist desc, c_count desc;
+---------+----------+
| c_count | custdist |
+---------+----------+
| 0       | 50005    |
| 9       | 6641     |
| 10      | 6532     |
| 11      | 6014     |
| 8       | 5937     |
| 12      | 5639     |
| 13      | 5024     |
| 19      | 4793     |
| 7       | 4687     |

| 20      | 4516     |

| 14      | 4446     |

| 21      | 4190     |

| 6       | 3265     |
| 23      | 3225     |
| 24      | 2742     |
| 25      | 2086     |
| 5       | 1948     |
| 26      | 1612     |
| 27      | 1179     |
| 4       | 1007     |
| 28      | 893      |

| 3       | 415      |
| 30      | 376      |
| 31      | 226      |
| 32      | 148      |

| 33      | 75       |

| 35      | 37       |
| 1       | 17       |
| 36      | 14       |
| 38      | 5        |
| 37      | 5        |
| 40      | 4        |
| 41      | 2        |
| 39      | 1        |
+---------+----------+
42 rows in set
```

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)
with/without tiflash get the same wrong result
```sql
[17:40:39]TiDB root:test> SELECT c_count, count(*) as custdist
                       -> from ( SELECT c_custkey, count(o_orderkey)  as  c_count
                       ->        from customer left join orders on c_custkey = o_custkey and o_comment not like '%special%requests%'
                       ->        group by c_custkey ) c_orders
                       -> group by c_count
                       -> order by custdist desc, c_count desc;
+---------+----------+
| c_count | custdist |
+---------+----------+
| 1       | 99995    |
| 0       | 50005    |
+---------+----------+
2 rows in set
Time: 0.111s
[17:43:33]TiDB root:test> explain analyze SELECT c_count, count(*) as custdist
                       -> from ( SELECT c_custkey, count(o_orderkey)  as  c_count
                       ->        from customer left join orders on c_custkey = o_custkey and o_comment not like '%special%requests%'
                       ->        group by c_custkey ) c_orders
                       -> group by c_count
                       -> order by custdist desc, c_count desc;
```

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)
v6.5.2, v7.1.0
```sql
[17:44:25]TiDB root:test> select tidb_version();
+-----------------------------------------------------------+

| Check Table Before Drop: false                            |
| Store: tikv                                               |
+-----------------------------------------------------------+
```
<!-- Paste the output of SELECT tidb_version() -->
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE customer (c_custkey INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, c_name VARCHAR(50), c_address VARCHAR(100), c_comment VARCHAR(200));
CREATE TABLE orders (o_orderkey INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, o_custkey INTEGER, o_comment VARCHAR(200), FOREIGN KEY (o_custkey) REFERENCES customer(c_custkey));

INSERT INTO customer (c_custkey, c_name, c_address, c_comment) VALUES (1, 'Alice', '123 Main St', 'No special requests'), (2, 'Bob', '456 Elm St', 'Special requests'), (3, 'Charlie', '789 Oak St', 'Regular customer');
INSERT INTO orders (o_orderkey, o_custkey, o_comment) VALUES (1, 1, 'Order 1'), (2, 1, 'Order 2'), (3, 2, 'Order 3'), (4, 3, 'Special requests'), (5, 1, 'Order 4');

SELECT c_count, COUNT(*) AS custdist FROM (SELECT c_custkey, COUNT(o_orderkey) AS c_count FROM customer LEFT JOIN orders ON c_custkey = o_custkey AND NOT CONTAINS(o_comment, 'special requests') GROUP BY c_custkey) c_orders GROUP BY c_count ORDER BY custdist DESC, c_count DESC;
